summary.intent: Audit the entire Moltis workspace and convert all library crates that used anyhow (without thiserror) to have proper thiserror-based Error types at their public API boundaries.
summary.outcome: All 8 offending library crates were converted to thiserror-based error types with typed Error enums, 367 tests pass, workspace compiles clean, and a PR was created with 70 files changed.

repo: CLAUDE.md policy: binary/app crates may use anyhow, library crates must use thiserror at API boundaries
AgentTool::execute trait (in moltis-agents) returns anyhow::Result, forcing crates that implement it (caldav, mcp-agent-bridge) to keep anyhow for trait impl return types
MemoryWriter trait (in moltis-agents) returns anyhow::Result, forcing memory and qmd crates to keep anyhow at trait impl boundaries
MemoryRuntime trait (in moltis-memory) returns anyhow::Result, further constraining qmd
Worktree-isolated subagents lose their changes to the parent worktree — committed changes in subagent worktrees do not propagate automatically
The moltis-tools crate had a pre-existing clippy failure in task_list.rs unrelated to this work
27 library crates were already correct (thiserror only), 11 were valid mixed usage (anyhow internally + thiserror at boundary), 3 app/binary crates correctly used anyhow

code.finding: Called SkillWatcher::start and needed update to handle new skills error type
Called node-host functions and needed update for new NodeHostError type
Test mock EmbeddingProvider impl was returning anyhow::Result instead of moltis_memory::error::Result
Test code implementing SkillDiscoverer needed to return new skills error type
Had unnecessary crate::error::Result qualification causing unused import lint warnings

workflow: Examining trait boundaries before writing error types is essential — trait return types constrain what error types are possible in implementing crates
Running subagents with worktree isolation loses changes unless the subagent explicitly commits; prefer non-isolated agents for implementation tasks
Batch parallel subagent execution works well for independent crates, but recovery when isolation fails requires a second pass without isolation
Downstream callers in other crates (cli, gateway, tools, chat) must be updated when public error types change — grep for all call sites before declaring done

friction: Worktree-isolated subagents committed changes to their own isolated branches but those changes didn't appear in the parent worktree — only auth was committed, httpd had partial working-tree changes, others were lost entirely
Required a full second pass (re-running all 6 missing crates) without worktree isolation after discovering the isolation problem
Multiple rounds of downstream compile errors in cli, gateway, tools, and chat test code that weren't caught until full workspace check
cargo fmt pulled in unrelated code-index formatting changes, inflating the diff
open_items: The AgentTool::execute, MemoryWriter, and MemoryRuntime traits in moltis-agents still return anyhow::Result — these should be migrated to typed errors to eliminate the anyhow dependency in implementing crates (caldav, mcp-agent-bridge, memory, qmd)
Pre-existing clippy failure in crates/tools/src/skill_tools/task_list.rs was not fixed as it was out of scope
